text:
Something quietly flipped in the data world this year, and most teams haven't caught up.

For the last decade, governance was the brake. Legal's problem. The thing that slowed your roadmap down right before a launch. Monetization was the gas pedal — new endpoints, new dashboards, new SKUs.

That model is dead. It just hasn't been buried yet.

Here's what I'm seeing: the companies actually shipping paid data products in 2026 aren't the ones with the most data. They're the ones who turned lineage, access control, and audit trails into features customers pay more for. Trust became a SKU. Governance became the product.

My 12-24 month call: the data monetization market is going to roughly 10x this decade, but the spoils won't split evenly. A huge chunk of governance programs will quietly fail — not because the tech is bad, but because they were scoped as compliance projects with no revenue line attached. Meanwhile, a smaller cohort will package governance primitives as premium tiers and watch ACVs climb.

If you're a PM sitting on a data product right now, the move isn't to ship faster. It's to go find your governance lead this week and ask: which of these controls would an enterprise buyer pay extra for? Then price them.

Treating governance as a cost center in 2026 is the new "we'll add SSO later."

What's the first governance capability you'd feel comfortable charging for?

#DataMonetization #DataGovernance #DataProducts #ProductStrategy #DaaS #B2BSaaS #DataStrategy
